purpose: Base modal wrapper component used as the foundation for nearly all modal dialogs in the app. Provides a fixed overlay div (.history-overlay), a centered modal panel (.history-modal) with a header (title + close X button), a scrollable body, and an optional fixed footer drawer below the body. Handles two universal close affordances: ESC key (global keydown listener attached/removed in useEffect) and overlay-only click (event.target === event.currentTarget guards against clicks bubbling from inner content). Sets ARIA attributes (role='dialog', aria-modal, aria-label) for accessibility. The title prop accepts both strings and React nodes — ErrorModal exploits this to inject colored span. The optional className is appended to .history-modal for per-modal sizing/styling overrides (e.g. 'commands-modal', 'logs-modal', 'agent-details-modal-wrapper').
gotchas: Class names are .history-overlay and .history-modal (legacy from when only HistoryModal existed) — NOT .modal-overlay. New CSS must target these names.

Title prop is rendered raw inside a span — passing JSX works (ErrorModal does this) but means there is no string fallback for aria-label when title is a React node; the aria-label receives the JSX object and may not render meaningfully to screen readers.

The close-on-outside-click only fires when e.target === e.currentTarget (the overlay div itself). Clicking on whitespace inside .history-modal does NOT close — only clicking the surrounding overlay backdrop does.
ESC handler is attached to document, not the modal — multiple stacked modals will all receive the ESC and may all close simultaneously.
Does NOT use React Portal — modal renders in the DOM where it is mounted. CSS z-index in styles must keep .history-overlay above other layers.
If onClose is null/undefined, the close button is hidden AND ESC has no effect — be deliberate about always passing onClose unless intentionally locking the modal.
patterns: Every other modal in this directory wraps its body in <Modal title=... onClose=... className=...> rather than reimplementing overlay/ESC/header logic — single source of truth for keyboard and click-out behavior.
Modals that need fixed action buttons at the bottom use the `footer` prop (e.g. ProjectModal); modals with inline buttons in the body do not (e.g. ConfirmModal, ErrorModal, TaskEditorModal).
conventions: Filename and component are PascalCase: Modal.jsx exports default Modal.
JSX comment header at top of file describes purpose in 1-3 lines.
JSDoc block above the component describes props.
